7500.label: Hijack
7500.tip: 7500 — Unlawful interference (hijack). ICAO worldwide emergency code; ATC treats as highest priority.

7600.label: Radio failure
7600.tip: 7600 — Radio communication failure. Pilot has lost two-way contact with ATC.

7700.label: General emergency
7700.tip: 7700 — General emergency. Used for any urgent situation requiring priority handling.

1200.label: VFR aeroplane
1200.tip: NZ: VFR civil aeroplanes when no discrete ATC code assigned. US/Canada: VFR conspicuity in uncontrolled airspace.

1177.label: VFR conspicuity (ICAO)
1177.tip: 1177 — VFR conspicuity code used in some ICAO regions when not assigned a discrete code.

7000.label: VFR conspicuity (Europe)
7000.tip: 7000 — VFR conspicuity in Europe when flying without an ATC-assigned code.

2000.label: IFR / Oceanic VFR
2000.tip: NZ: IFR in NZ FIR (or assigned code); VFR in Auckland Oceanic FIR. Elsewhere: IFR entering controlled airspace before receiving a discrete ATC squawk.

0111.label: Fire / recon (NZ)
0111.tip: NZ: Fire suppression and reconnaissance (fixed-wing and helicopters) per AIP NZ ENR 1.6.

0021.label: VFR below FL195 (UK)
0021.tip: 0021 — UK: VFR flight not above FL195.

0023.label: VFR military / special (UK)
0023.tip: 0023 — UK: VFR military or special operations.

0033.label: Parachute operations
0033.tip: 0033 — Parachute dropping in progress; alerts ATC and nearby traffic.

0036.label: Military low-level / formation
0036.tip: 0036 — UK: military low-level or formation flying.

0050.label: ATC assigned (UK)
0050.tip: 0050 — UK: reserved band for ATC-assigned codes in some FIRs.

0066.label: Glider – left-hand circuit (UK)
0066.tip: 0066 — UK: glider flying a left-hand circuit.

0070.label: VFR conspicuity (UK)
0070.tip: 0070 — UK: VFR conspicuity when not receiving a discrete ATC assignment.

0735.label: CCAMS assigned
0735.tip: 0735 — UK/Europe: discrete code allocated by CCAMS (Centralised Code Assignment) for en-route ATC identification. Range 0730–0767.

1300.label: Glider / balloon (NZ)
1300.tip: NZ: VFR gliders and balloons when no discrete ATC code assigned.

1400.label: GA areas (NZ)
1400.tip: NZ: Powered aircraft in designated general aviation areas. (UK uses 1400 for FIS listening squawk.)

1500.label: Helicopter VFR (NZ)
1500.tip: NZ: Civil helicopters when no discrete ATC code assigned.

2200.label: Controlled circuit (NZ)
2200.tip: NZ: VFR aircraft in the aerodrome traffic circuit at a controlled aerodrome.

4000.label: Military intercept / handoff
4000.tip: 4000 — Military intercept operations or negotiating transfer to another ATC unit.

4400.label: DVFR (UK)
4400.tip: 4400 — UK: defence VFR flight rules traffic.

6000.label: Defence / military
6000.tip: NZ: Defence aeroplanes (VFR). Also used elsewhere for military operations.

6500.label: Defence heli (NZ)
6500.tip: NZ: Defence helicopters when no discrete ATC code assigned.
